data.narrative:
QuEra Computing is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $230M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable entities: 3 publicly traded firms (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private companies including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method here. Using total capital raised of $230M as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $437M. This method is grounded in observed progression patterns across frontier-tech funding rounds. Peer-median triangulation cannot be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within quantum computing—while five sector peers exist, none meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints in narrow stage or valuation bands. Relying on all-sector median comparables would introduce material noise given the heterogeneity of quantum platforms and development timelines.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $371M (low) to $503M (high), with a median point of $437M. This range reflects a single-method triangulation and carries material uncertainty around the appropriate Series B step-up multiplier for frontier-stage quantum hardware. Key risks include the early-stage nature of quantum utility realization, customer concentration, and the absence of tighter peer comparables at QuEra's specific stage and technical positioning.
